steps.title: Factor the left side
steps.content: Rewrite \(-3x + 21px = 84\) by factoring x: $$(-3 + 21p)x = 84$$ A linear equation of the form \(ax = b\) has no solution when \(a = 0\) and \(b \neq 0\) (the equation becomes \(0 = 84\), a contradiction). So we need \(-3 + 21p = 0\).

steps.title: Solve for p and test all choices
steps.content:
$$21p = 3 \implies p = \frac{3}{21} = \frac{1}{7}$$
  • Choice A — \(0\): \(p=0\) gives \(-3x = 84\), which has solution \(x = -28\). One solution, not no solution. ✗
  • Choice B — \(\frac{1}{7}\): \(-3 + 21 \cdot \frac{1}{7} = -3 + 3 = 0\), so \(0x = 84\) — no solution. ✓
  • Choice C — \(\frac{4}{3}\): \(-3 + 21 \cdot \frac{4}{3} = -3 + 28 = 25 \neq 0\), so one solution exists. ✗
  • Choice D — \(4\): \(-3 + 21 \cdot 4 = -3 + 84 = 81 \neq 0\), so one solution exists. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
